title: Kozo Okamoto, member of Japanese Red Army wanted for Lod massacre, dies in Lebanon
summary: The 78-year-old terrorist, who participated in the 1972 attack at Tel Aviv airport in which 26 people died, died in Beirut, according to Japanese authorities.

title: Bill for 'secondary capital' stalls in Diet as opposition refuses to vote
summary: Opposition parties refuse to participate in voting on the bill that seeks to decentralize Tokyo's functions, demanding more discussion and a referendum.

title: Former Japanese volleyball player admits cannabis possession in first court appearance
summary: Shunichiro Sato, 29, former member of the national team, pleaded guilty and said he 'crossed the line a bit' during a training camp.

title: Japan tightens immigration control with more officers and measures against illegal stay
summary: The government decided to increase the immigration office workforce by 230 and will establish a committee of experts to set quotas for accepting foreigners.

title: Japan urges Google, X and three other companies to comply with platform transparency law
summary: The Ministry of Internal Affairs issued formal recommendations due to deficiencies in the disclosure of information on advertising and content moderation.

title: Japan enacts first reform of Imperial House Law to ensure dynastic succession
summary: The amendment allows women of the imperial family to retain their status after marriage, but postpones discussion on succession to the throne through the female line.

title: Japan records fourth consecutive day of extreme temperatures above 40°C
summary: The city of Gifu reached 40.2 degrees and dozens of heatstroke cases were reported, as the heat wave alert continues.

title: Japan to establish National Intelligence Office with Kazuya Hara as first director
summary: The new body will centralize foreign and domestic intelligence functions, and will start operations on July 31.

title: United States raises tariffs on Japan to 12.5% in new trade offensive
summary: The Trump administration activated the tariff measure it had announced, raising the rate from the previous 10%, causing concern in the Japanese economy.

title: Nikkei index falls 1,811 points dragged by tech stocks collapse
summary: The Tokyo stock market closed in the red for the fourth consecutive day, with widespread losses led by semiconductor stocks.

title: Prudential insurance scandal expands with 125 new whistleblowers and criticism over opacity
summary: The insurer faces allegations of improper sales and refuses to give explanations at a press conference, as the number of victims grows.

title: Japan finds rich deposits of heavy rare earths on seabed off Minami-Torishima island
summary: The extracted samples contain more than 50% heavy rare earth elements, essential for manufacturing high-tech devices.

title: Fukuoka Assembly vice-chairman resigns over corruption allegations
summary: Yuki Nakao submitted his resignation following reports of irregular financing, although he maintained that 'nobody trusts me.'

title: Father arrested for murdering his 12-year-old daughter in Nagano
summary: The 38-year-old man, who had been previously detained for injuring his wife, was charged with the homicide of the minor.

title: Japanese writer Toshiichi Akamatsu dies at 70
summary: The novelist, known for his provocative style and debut with 'Seaweed and Crabs', died according to those close to him.

title: Scandal at Japan Finance Corporation: ex-employee arrested for bribery and vice president apologizes
summary: The public credit institution created a special committee to investigate the case of a former employee who allegedly received luxury watches in exchange for loans.

title: Woman injured by hunter's gunshot while driving in Fukuoka
summary: The stray bullet hit the vehicle and caused an injury to the driver's ear, who was taken to a hospital.

title: Trump threatens 'biggest attack' on Iran if it does not stop supporting Houthis
summary: The US president said Iran 'has not suffered enough yet' and is considering a massive offensive, while Iran denies the accusations.

title: Trump calls Shohei Ohtani a 'legend' during Dodgers visit to White House
summary: The president praised the Japanese baseball player and also pitcher Yoshinobu Yamamoto for his 'incredible' performance.

title: Japan Football Association weighs continuity of Hajime Moriyasu as head coach
summary: After initially telling him he would only be in charge until the World Cup, officials are considering extending the coach's contract.

title: China and United States pave way for summit between Xi Jinping and Trump
summary: China's vice foreign minister met with the US deputy secretary of state to prepare for possible Xi visit to Washington in September.

title: Naomi Umeno, Japan's first female prosecutor general, leaves post and is succeeded by Ryuji Kawabara
summary: Umeno retires after a historic career, and her successor will take up the post at the Tokyo High Public Prosecutors Office.

title: Defense minister denies Self-Defense Forces spied on citizens' ideologies
summary: Kazuo Koizumi said there were no inappropriate activities, after reports surfaced about an intelligence unit investigating civilians' patriotism.

title: White House withdraws subpoena against New York Times journalist
summary: The decision came after the judge strongly questioned the legality of the request against the reporter covering a document leak.

title: Japan to change English name of its Upper House to 'Senate' to avoid confusion
summary: The name 'House of Councillors' will be replaced by 'Senate' from a date yet to be determined.
